analysis.consistency: The entry is consistent with standard D&D 5e class feature documentation. It clearly defines when the feature is gained (1st level) and how it scales (through the Cleric table, referencing the spell list).
analysis.quality: High quality. The description is concise and directly references external, standard tables/lists ('cleric spell list', 'Cleric table'), which is typical for rules text.

mechanics.core_effect: Grants the character knowledge of a set number of cantrips at 1st level (three), which increases at higher levels.
mechanics.scaling: Scaling is based on the character's overall class level, tracked via the 'Cantrips Known' column on the Cleric table.
mechanics.prerequisites: Requires the character to be a Cleric.
